senses.definition: a tool with a metal head and a handle, used for striking nails, etc.

examples.x: He used a hammer to drive nails into the wooden frame.
She carefully tapped the hammer to avoid splitting the wood.
He kept a hammer in his toolbox for various home repair tasks.
The hammer's weight provided the force needed for tougher jobs.

senses.definition: a hammer-like tool with a large wooden or rubber head used for striking or directing objects

examples.x: The carpenter used a wooden mallet to gently tap the chisel into the wood.
The sculptor carefully tapped the marble block with a small mallet to shape the sculpture.
The blacksmith wielded a sturdy metal mallet to shape the red-hot iron into horseshoes.

senses.definition: a metal tool with a toothed blade that is used for cutting wood, metal, etc. by moving back and forth

examples.x: He used a saw to cut the wooden board to the desired length.
He carefully measured before using the saw to ensure accuracy.
The saw's sharp teeth helped it glide smoothly through the material.
She checked the blade of the saw for any signs of wear before starting.
He kept the saw in a secure place when not in use to prevent accidents.

senses.definition: a tool with a toothed chain inside that is powered by a small engine, used for cutting wood

examples.x: He used a chainsaw to cut down the old tree in his yard.
The chainsaw's powerful motor made quick work of the thick logs.
She wore protective gear while using the chainsaw for safety.
The chainsaw came with safety features to prevent accidents.
He maintained the chainsaw by regularly sharpening the chain.

senses.definition: a handheld tool that uses rotational force to create holes or drive screws in various materials

examples.x: The carpenter used a cordless drill to assemble the wooden shelves.
He adjusted the speed settings on the drill to ensure precise drilling into the metal.
The construction crew relied on powerful drills to anchor the beams securely into the foundation.

senses.definition: a hand tool with a handle and a jaw or jaws designed to grip, turn, or hold objects such as nuts, bolts, or pipes

examples.x: He used a wrench to tighten the bolts on the bicycle's handlebars.
She needed a larger wrench to loosen the rusted nuts on the car's wheel.
The assembly line worker used a wrench to secure the components of the machine.

senses.definition: a small pointy piece of metal that can be fasten into wooden or metal objects using a screwdriver to hold things together

examples.x: He used a screwdriver to tighten the screws holding the shelves to the wall.
The carpenter inserted screws into the wooden boards to construct the frame.
She tightened the screws on the chair to prevent it from wobbling.
The technician used a screwdriver to remove the screws holding the panel in place.
He replaced the old screws with longer ones to better secure the shelf to the wall.

senses.definition: a small tool with a metal part by which screws can be turned

examples.x: He grabbed a screwdriver to assemble the new furniture.
The set included different types of screwdrivers for various screw heads.
He kept a screwdriver in his toolbox for quick repairs around the house.
The magnetic tip of the screwdriver helped hold screws in place.

senses.definition: a small strong pointy metal that is inserted into walls or wooden objects using a hammer to hang things from or fasten them together
a substance that is used to stick things to each other

examples.x: She applied glue to the edges of the paper to create a scrapbook.
The glue held the pieces of the broken vase together securely.
She kept a bottle of glue handy for school projects and repairs.
The glue's strong formula made it ideal for tough materials like metal.
He made sure to let the glue dry completely before using the item again.

senses.definition: a metal tool with a rough surface that is used to smooth wooden or metal rough edges

examples.x: She used a metal file to sharpen the blades of her gardening shears.
The sculptor employed a fine-grit file to refine the contours of the clay sculpture.
He used a diamond file to precisely shape the metal components of the jewelry.
The mechanic used a half-round file to deburr the edges of the metal part.

senses.definition: a metal tool with a handle and a strong flat-edged blade that is used to shape hard objects, such as wood, metal, etc.

examples.x: He used a chisel to carve intricate designs into the wood.
The stonemason relied on a chisel to shape the marble blocks.
She learned how to use a chisel safely in her woodworking class.
The chisel's sharp edge made it effective for detailed work.
The set included different sizes of chisels for various tasks.

senses.definition: a piece of metal like a thick nail without a point that used to secure assembled parts by passing through holes and tightening with a nut

examples.x: I need to tighten the bolt on the bicycle wheel.
Make sure to secure the furniture to the wall with bolts.
Please hand me the wrench so I can loosen the bolt.
The construction worker used a heavy-duty bolt to connect the steel beams.
The mechanic replaced the rusted bolt with a new one.

senses.definition: a flat piece of metal with a hole in the middle through which a bolt is put to secure or fasten objects together

examples.x: He tightened the nut onto the bolt to secure the bracket firmly in place.
The mechanic used a wrench to loosen the nut holding the wheel hub.
She replaced the missing nut on the bolt to prevent the assembly from coming loose.

senses.definition: a flat rubber, plastic, or metal ring which is small and acts as a seal or is put between a nut and a bolt to tighten their connection

examples.x: He used a washer to secure the bolt on the lawnmower.
The washer helps prevent leaks in the plumbing system.
She replaced the old washer to fix the dripping faucet.
The set included various sizes of washers for different projects.
A rubber washer is often used in plumbing to create a watertight seal.

senses.definition: a gardening tool with a handle and three or four sharp points, used for digging or moving hay

examples.x: The fork's tines were perfect for breaking up compacted dirt.
She enjoyed using a fork to mix compost into her garden bed.
He found the fork useful for turning over leaves in the fall.
He relied on the fork to mix soil amendments into his potting mix.
She used the fork to prepare the vegetable patch for planting.

senses.definition: a tool that has a long handle with a broad curved metal end, used for moving snow, soil, etc.

examples.x: He found a lightweight shovel useful for camping trips.
The shovel was perfect for digging holes for planting trees.
She always kept a shovel in the shed for yard work.
He grabbed a shovel to clear the driveway after the snowstorm.
The shovel's sharp edge made it easier to cut through tough ground.

senses.definition: an object with two handles and one wheel, used for carrying things

examples.x: He filled the wheelbarrow with soil for the garden.
The construction crew used a wheelbarrow to move bricks around the site.
She pushed the wheelbarrow carefully over the uneven ground.
The wheelbarrow made it easier to transport heavy items to the backyard.
She decorated her wheelbarrow for a fun garden display.

senses.definition: a portable, often metal box for organizing and keeping tools in

examples.x: He kept his wrenches, screwdrivers, and pliers neatly organized in his toolbox.
The handyman carried his toolbox to the job site to have all the necessary tools at hand.
She rummaged through the toolbox to find the right size socket wrench for the project.
The mechanic's toolbox contained specialized tools for automotive repairs.
The carpenter's toolbox was a well-worn wooden chest filled with saws, hammers, and measuring tapes.

senses.definition: a small metal tool with two jaws used for gripping, bending, or cutting materials such as wires, pipes, or small objects

examples.x: The electrician used pliers to twist the wires together securely.
He used needle-nose pliers to reach into tight spaces and grasp small components.
The plumber used channel-lock pliers to tighten the pipe fittings.
She used wire-cutting pliers to trim the excess wire from the electrical circuit.
The jeweler used precision pliers to manipulate delicate pieces of metal for crafting jewelry.

senses.definition: a hand tool specifically designed for cutting wires and cables

examples.x: He used a wire cutter to trim the excess electrical wire after completing the installation.
The electrician relied on wire cutters to neatly cut through the thick cables.
She used a pair of diagonal wire cutters to snip the damaged wire and replace it with a new one.
The hobbyist used wire cutters to shape and trim the wires for his DIY electronics project.
The maintenance technician carried a set of wire cutters in his toolkit for repairing electrical equipment.

senses.definition: a silver-colored object that is sticky on one side and is used for fixing things

examples.x: He used duct tape to temporarily patch the hole in the inflatable boat.
The handyman used duct tape to secure the loose wires behind the television.
She wrapped duct tape around the broken handle of her suitcase to keep it together.
The mechanic used duct tape to fix a tear in the car's upholstery.
During camping, duct tape proved invaluable for repairing torn tents and broken equipment.

senses.definition: a tool with a rubber cup fixed to a handle, used for clearing blocked pipes or drains

examples.x: He used a plunger to unclog the kitchen sink after it became blocked with food debris.
The plumber recommended using a plunger to clear the toilet blockage.
She kept a plunger handy in the bathroom cabinet for minor plumbing emergencies.
The maintenance staff used a plunger to clear the drain in the shower room.
The homeowner successfully used a plunger to fix the slow-draining bathtub.

senses.definition: an iron bar with one end curved that is used as a lever

examples.x: He used a crowbar to pry open the old wooden door.
He kept a crowbar in his garage for various projects.
She learned how to handle a crowbar safely to avoid injury.

senses.definition: a metal tool that uses staples to fix paper or wood to other materials

examples.x: He used a staple gun to attach the fabric to the wooden frame.
She demonstrated how to use the staple gun safely to avoid accidents.
He carefully loaded the staple gun before starting his craft project.
The staple gun is commonly used in upholstery projects.
The manual staple gun is lightweight and easy to handle.

senses.definition: a small razor with an adjustable blade used for cutting cardboard boxes, packaging materials, and other items

examples.x: He used a box cutter to open the package that had just arrived.
She used a box cutter to carefully slice through the wrapping paper on her birthday present.
The contractor used a sturdy box cutter to cut drywall sheets to size during the renovation.
The artist used a box cutter to precisely cut out shapes from cardboard for a sculpture project.

senses.definition: to work or perform properly

examples.x: Regular maintenance is crucial to ensure that your car continues to function smoothly.
After a thorough cleaning, the computer's operating system began to function more responsively.
A well-balanced diet is essential for the human body to function at its best.
The organization implemented new policies to ensure that its processes would function more efficiently.

senses.definition: a type of wrench with a movable part that can turn or hold things of different sizes

examples.x: He used an adjustable wrench to tighten the plumbing fittings.
The adjustable wrench is a must-have in any toolbox for repairs.
She appreciated how the adjustable wrench could replace multiple fixed-size wrenches.
The adjustable wrench is useful for both DIY tasks and professional work.
She found the adjustable wrench handy for assembling furniture with different bolt sizes.

senses.definition: a flexible measuring tool consisting of a long strip of metal, cloth, or plastic with measurement markings, used to measure lengths and distances accurately

examples.x: The carpenter used a tape measure to determine the length of the wooden board needed for the shelf.
She checked the dimensions of the room with a tape measure before buying new furniture.
The tailor used a tape measure to take precise measurements for the custom-made suit.
He marked the positions on the wall with a pencil based on the measurements from the tape measure.
The surveyor used a laser tape measure for accurate distance measurements in the field.

senses.definition: a large tool consisted of a long handle with a heavy metal block at its end, used with both hands to break a stone, etc.

examples.x: He used a sledgehammer to break apart the old concrete patio.
The construction crew relied on a sledgehammer for heavy demolition work.
She found the sledgehammer effective for removing stubborn tiles.
He swung the sledgehammer with precision to avoid damaging nearby structures.
The sledgehammer's weight made it ideal for delivering powerful strikes.

senses.definition: a tool with a long wooden handle attached to a heavy steel or iron blade, primarily used for chopping wood and cutting down trees

examples.x: He used an ax to chop firewood for the winter.
The ax had a sharp blade that made cutting easier.
She learned how to handle an ax safely during the camping trip.
The lumberjack carried his ax as he worked in the forest.
He polished the wooden handle of his grandfather's old ax.
